title: EVENT-BASED MULTIMODAL UNDERSTANDING CHALLENGE: GENERAL UNDERSTANDING TRACK @ECCV 2026
description: Evaluates general dynamic scene understanding from event streams, covering detailed understanding, causal reasoning, action recognition, gesture recognition and Event OCR.
description_zh: 评估事件流中的一般动态场景理解能力，涵盖详细理解、因果推理、行动识别、手势识别和事件光学字符识别。

pages.title: Overview
pages.content:
# EVENT-BASED MULTIMODAL UNDERSTANDING CHALLENGE: GENERAL UNDERSTANDING TRACK @ECCV 2026

Welcome to the Event Understanding for Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) Challenge, officially hosted at ECCV 2026! 

## 🎯 Overview & Objective
This challenge aims to systematically evaluate and push the boundaries of MLLMs in understanding and reasoning over event-based vision data. Unlike traditional RGB cameras, event cameras capture asynchronous brightness changes with high temporal resolution and high dynamic range. Our goal is to foster the development of robust, parameter-efficient multi-modal models capable of deep semantic comprehension, spatial-temporal reasoning, and fine-grained recognition within complex event streams.

## 📝 Task Description
To comprehensively benchmark model capabilities, the challenge evaluates performance across 8 distinct core tasks:

* **Detailed Understanding**: Comprehending fine-grained visual details, motions, and complex interactions within event streams.
* **Causal Reasoning**: Inferring the cause-and-effect relationships and physical logic between dynamic events.
* **Action Recognition**: Accurately identifying and categorizing human actions or dynamic activities over time.
* **Gesture Recognition**: Recognizing specific hand gestures and micro-motions from high-speed event signals.
* **Event OCR**: Detecting, extracting, and reading text content captured under challenging lighting or fast-motion scenarios.

## 📅 Important Dates
*All deadlines are at 11:59 PM UTC.*

* **May 10, 2026**: Challenge Website Opens.
* **May 12, 2026**: Training & Validation Data Released.
* **May 20, 2026**: Validation Server Online.
* **June 25, 2026**: Test Data Released & Test Server Online.
* **July 3, 2026**: Test Submission Deadline & Test Server Closes.
* **July 10, 2026**: Results Announcement *(Tentative)*.
* **TBD**: Technical Report Submission.

## 📊 Data Statistics (Test Set)
The benchmark encompasses a rigorously annotated test set spanning the following distributions:

| Task Category | Number of Items |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Gesture Recognition** | 894 |
| **Detailed Understanding** | 483 |
| **Action Recognition** | 365 |
| **Casual Reasoning** | 180 |
| **Event OCR** | 162 |

## 🔗 Provided Resources
To help you get started and build your models, please refer to our official resources:
* **Training Data**: [Download via ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/XduSyL/EventMind/files)
* **Academic Paper**: [Read on arXiv (2511.18448)](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.18448)
* **Project Homepage**: [Visit EventBench Website](https://xdusyl.github.io/eventbench.github.io/)

## ⚠️ Challenge Rules
To maintain fairness and scientific integrity, all participants must strictly adhere to the following rules:
1. **No Cheating or Probing**: Any attempt to probe the test server, manipulate submissions to extract test set answers, or use the test data for model training is strictly prohibited and will result in immediate disqualification.
2. **Model Size Constraint**: To encourage efficient and accessible model designs, the total parameter count of any evaluated model must be **<= 10B** (10 Billion parameters).
pages.content_zh:
# 基于事件的多模态理解挑战：通用理解赛道 @ECCV 2026

欢迎来到事件理解多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）挑战赛，由ECCV 2026官方主办！

## 🎯 概述与目标
本挑战旨在系统性评估并推进MLLMs在理解和推理基于事件的视觉数据方面的能力边界。与传统RGB摄像机不同，事件摄像机以高时间分辨率和高动态范围捕捉异步亮度变化。我们的目标是促进开发健壮、参数高效的多模态模型，具备深层语义理解、时空推理和复杂事件流中的细粒度识别能力。

## 📝 任务描述
为全面评测模型能力，本挑战赛通过8个不同的核心任务进行评估：

* **细节理解**：理解事件流中的细粒度视觉细节、运动和复杂交互。
* **因果推理**：推断动态事件之间的因果关系和物理逻辑。
* **动作识别**：准确识别和分类人类动作或随时间变化的动态活动。
* **手势识别**：从高速事件信号中识别特定的手部手势和微动作。
* **事件OCR**：在具有挑战性的光照或快速运动场景中检测、提取和读取文本内容。

## 📅 重要日期
*所有截止时间均为UTC时间晚上11:59。*

* **2026年5月10日**：挑战赛网站开放。
* **2026年5月12日**：训练和验证数据发布。
* **2026年5月20日**：验证服务器上线。
* **2026年6月25日**：测试数据发布与测试服务器上线。
* **2026年7月3日**：测试提交截止与测试服务器关闭。
* **2026年7月10日**：结果公布*（暂定）*。
* **待定**：技术报告提交。

## 📊 数据统计（测试集）
该基准包含跨越以下分布的严格标注测试集：

| 任务类别 | 项目数量 |
| :--- | :--- |
| **手势识别** | 894 |
| **细节理解** | 483 |
| **动作识别** | 365 |
| **因果推理** | 180 |
| **事件OCR** | 162 |

## 🔗 提供的资源
为帮助您快速开始和构建模型，请参考我们的官方资源：
* **训练数据**：[通过ModelScope下载](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/XduSyL/EventMind/files)
* **学术论文**：[在arXiv上阅读（2511.18448）](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2511.18448)
* **项目主页**：[访问EventBench网站](https://xdusyl.github.io/eventbench.github.io/)

## ⚠️ 挑战赛规则
为维护公平性和科学诚信，所有参赛者必须严格遵守以下规则：
1. **禁止作弊或探测**：严格禁止任何试图探测测试服务器、通过操纵提交来提取测试集答案或使用测试数据进行模型训练的行为，违规者将被立即取消资格。
2. **模型规模限制**：为鼓励高效且易于获取的模型设计，任何评估模型的总参数数必须**≤ 10B**（100亿参数）。

pages.title: Data Preparation
pages.content:
# Data Preparation Guide

To participate in the EventBench challenge, you will need to acquire both the training data and the test set data (event streams and questions). Please follow the steps below to prepare your local environment.

## Step 1: Download the Training Set
The official training dataset, **EventMind**, is hosted on ModelScope. You can download the complete training split from the following link:

👉 **[Download EventMind Training Set (ModelScope)](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/XduSyL/EventMind)**

## Step 2: Download the Test Set (Event Streams & Questions)
The test set for the EventBench challenge consists of raw event stream files (`.npz`) and the corresponding test questions (`.json`). Due to file size constraints, these are hosted on Hugging Face.

You must download the **EventBench** dataset from the official repository:

👉 **[Download EventBench Test Set (Hugging Face)](https://huggingface.co/datasets/XduSyL/EventBench)**

*(Note: The test questions provided in the `input_data.zip` file directly on CodaBench do not contain the large `.npz` event files. You must download the event streams from the Hugging Face link above.)*

## Step 3: Detailed Instructions & Setup
For detailed instructions on data structure, how to read the `.npz` files, and how to set up the data pipeline for your model, please refer to the official EventBench project homepage.

👉 **[Visit the Official EventBench Project Homepage](https://xdusyl.github.io/eventbench.github.io/)**

The project page contains comprehensive documentation, code snippets, and baseline model examples to help you seamlessly integrate the data into your training and evaluation workflows.
pages.content_zh:
# 数据准备指南

为了参加EventBench竞赛，您需要获取训练数据和测试集数据（事件流和问题）。请按照以下步骤准备本地环境。

## 步骤1：下载训练集
官方训练数据集**EventMind**托管在ModelScope上。您可以从以下链接下载完整的训练集：

👉 **[下载EventMind训练集（ModelScope）](https://modelscope.cn/datasets/XduSyL/EventMind)**

## 步骤2：下载测试集（事件流和问题）
EventBench竞赛的测试集包括原始事件流文件（`.npz`）和相应的测试问题（`.json`）。由于文件大小限制，这些文件托管在Hugging Face上。

您必须从官方仓库下载**EventBench**数据集：

👉 **[下载EventBench测试集（Hugging Face）](https://huggingface.co/datasets/XduSyL/EventBench)**

*（注意：CodaBench上直接提供的`input_data.zip`文件中包含的测试问题不包含大型`.npz`事件文件。您必须从上面的Hugging Face链接下载事件流。）*

## 步骤3：详细说明和设置
有关数据结构的详细说明、如何读取`.npz`文件以及如何为您的模型设置数据管道的详细信息，请参考官方EventBench项目主页。

👉 **[访问官方EventBench项目主页](https://xdusyl.github.io/eventbench.github.io/)**

项目页面包含全面的文档、代码片段和基准模型示例，以帮助您无缝地将数据集成到您的训练和评估工作流程中。

pages.title: Evaluation Details
pages.content:
# Evaluation Metrics & Submission Guidelines

## Submission Format
Your submission must be a single JSON file. The file must contain a **flat dictionary** (key-value pairs), where the key is the test question `id` and the value is your model's predicted `answer`. 

*(Note: Please ensure your submission is a direct JSON object `{...}` and NOT wrapped in a JSON array `[{...}]` to ensure compatibility with our scoring engine.)*

**Example of `predictions.json`:**
```json
{
  "228350c5-4a8f-4976-abf9-fe52b86bddae": "D",
  "a959e02d-7ef6-457b-9a16-9c85934ba980": "5.3198",
  "e495b370-59ba-471b-8642-5ea239380f5a": "4",
  "d42e3a21-2c72-48ef-9f33-522338dc1c44": "left",
  "97258721-3d86-4bc2-82d1-481b59e1b7cd": "spatial\ntemporal"
}
```

## How to Submit
1. **Prepare your file:** Format your model's predictions and save them strictly as `predictions.json`.
2. **Compress:** Zip the `predictions.json` file directly into a `.zip` archive (e.g., `submission.zip`). **Do not** include any parent folders in the archive.
3. **Upload:** Navigate to the **"My Submissions"** tab on the challenge page, select the active phase, and upload your `.zip` file. The evaluation server will process your results automatically.

## Metrics
The challenge evaluates models across 5 distinct tasks. The evaluation server calculates the accuracy for each category based on the following specific matching rules:

* **Detailed Understanding**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Action Recognition**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Gesture Recognition**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Casual Reasoning**: Single-choice question. **Metric:** Exact match (A, B, C, or D).
* **Event OCR**: Character matching. **Metric:** Exact match required. *(Note: Please use the newline character `\n` to represent line breaks, e.g., `spatial\ntemporal`)*.

## Final Scoring
The evaluation script will independently calculate the individual accuracy scores for all 5 tasks. The final leaderboard ranking is strictly determined by the **Average Score**, which is the mean of the scores across all task categories.
pages.content_zh:
# 评估指标与提交指南

## 提交格式
您的提交必须是一个单一的 JSON 文件。该文件必须包含一个**平面字典**（键值对），其中键是测试问题的 `id`，值是您的模型预测的 `answer`。

*(注意：请确保您的提交是直接的 JSON 对象 `{...}`，而不是包装在 JSON 数组 `[{...}]` 中，以确保与我们的评分引擎的兼容性。)*

**`predictions.json` 示例：**
```json
{
  "228350c5-4a8f-4976-abf9-fe52b86bddae": "D",
  "a959e02d-7ef6-457b-9a16-9c85934ba980": "5.3198",
  "e495b370-59ba-471b-8642-5ea239380f5a": "4",
  "d42e3a21-2c72-48ef-9f33-522338dc1c44": "left",
  "97258721-3d86-4bc2-82d1-481b59e1b7cd": "spatial\ntemporal"
}
```

## 提交方式
1. **准备文件：** 格式化您的模型预测结果，并将其严格保存为 `predictions.json`。
2. **压缩：** 将 `predictions.json` 文件直接压缩为 `.zip` 存档（例如 `submission.zip`）。**不要** 在存档中包含任何父文件夹。
3. **上传：** 导航到竞赛页面的 **"我的提交"** 标签页，选择活跃阶段，并上传您的 `.zip` 文件。评分服务器将自动处理您的结果。

## 指标
本竞赛在 5 个不同任务中对模型进行评估。评分服务器根据以下特定匹配规则计算每个类别的准确度：

* **细节理解**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **动作识别**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **手势识别**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **因果推理**：单选题。**指标：** 完全匹配（A、B、C 或 D）。
* **事件 OCR**：字符匹配。**指标：** 需要完全匹配。*(注意：请使用换行符 `\n` 表示换行，例如 `spatial\ntemporal`)*。

## 最终得分
评分脚本将独立计算所有 5 个任务的个别准确度分数。最终排行榜排名完全由**平均分数**决定，即所有任务类别分数的平均值。

pages.title: Organizers & Citation
pages.content:
# Organizers & Citation

## Organizing Committee
We would like to thank the organizing committee for their efforts in creating and maintaining the EventBench challenge:

**Shaoyu Liu** (Xidian University) · liusy@stu.xidian.edu.cn  

**Hanyu Zhou** (National University of Singapore) · hy.zhou@nus.edu.sg

**Peiqi Duan** (Peking University) · duanqi0001@pku.edu.cn  

**Shihan Peng** (Huazhong University of Science and Technology) · pengshihan@hust.edu.cn

**Boxin Shi** (Peking University) · shiboxin@pku.edu.cn  

**Kuk-Jin Yoon** (KAIST) · kjyoon@kaist.ac.kr  

**Hui Xiong** (HKUST (Guangzhou)) · xionghui@ust.hk  

**Gim Hee Lee** (National University of Singapore) · gimhee.lee@nus.edu.sg  

**Davide Scaramuzza** (University of Zurich) · sdavide@ifi.uzh.ch 

## Contact Us
For any technical issues, questions about the dataset, or general inquiries regarding the challenge, please feel free to reach out to the organizers via the email addresses provided above.

## Citation
If you participate in this challenge or find the EventBench dataset useful for your research, please kindly cite our paper:
```bibtex
@article{liu2025eventbench,
  title={EventBench: Towards Comprehensive Benchmarking of Event-based MLLMs},
  author={Liu, Shaoyu and Li, Jianing and Zhao, Guanghui and Zhang, Yunjian and Ji, Xiangyang},
  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2511.18448},
  year={2025}
}
```

```bibtex
@inproceedings{liu2025eventgpt,
  title={Eventgpt: Event stream understanding with multimodal large language models},
  author={Liu, Shaoyu and Li, Jianing and Zhao, Guanghui and Zhang, Yunjian and Meng, Xin and Yu, Fei Richard and Ji, Xiangyang and Li, Ming},
  booktitle={Proceedings of the Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Conference},
  pages={29139--29149},
  year={2025}
}
```
